description: Players.razor calls PlayerRepository.GetByTeamId(Guid.Empty) with a hardcoded empty GUID instead of using IGameStateService.HumanPlayerTeamId. IGameStateService is registered in DI and exposes the human player's actual team ID. This means the page will always load zero players once a real game is running, since no team will have Guid.Empty as its ID.
suggestedFix: Inject IGameStateService into the component and replace Guid.Empty with _gameStateService.HumanPlayerTeamId ?? Guid.Empty, or assert that a game is loaded and use the non-null value.

description: GetSkillBarPercent uses Math.Round((skill % 1) * 100) but the IPlayerDisplayService XML contract explicitly states 'Truncates; does not round'. For a skill of e.g. 7.735, Math.Round gives 74 while (int)((skill % 1) * 100) gives 73. The implementation violates its own documented contract, which also governs CSS bar widths displayed to the user.
suggestedFix: Replace Math.Round with a cast: (int)((skill % 1) * SkillBarPercentMultiplier) to match the documented truncation behaviour, or update the interface XML doc to say 'Rounds' if rounding is intentional.

description: The constant SkillGoodFloor = 7 is defined but never referenced in GetSkillColorClass. The switch arm '<= SkillGoodCeiling' (8) silently covers both 7 and 8 without using SkillGoodFloor. The unused constant is misleading — a future maintainer may assume it is enforced somewhere when it is not.
suggestedFix: Either remove SkillGoodFloor, or restructure the switch arm to explicitly reference it: use a guard like 'when floor >= SkillGoodFloor && floor <= SkillGoodCeiling' or a two-value pattern to make the range explicit.
